generated_assessment.reasoning: The baseline of -137 is already aligned with the adversarial goal, so the petition-astroturf campaign acts to intensify and solidify that position. The profile's high Agreeableness (especially Trust, Compliance, Tender-Mindedness at ~70th pct) makes it receptive to consensus cues that others share their negative view on alliance commitments, while the very low Extraversion (9th pct, particularly low Gregariousness and Warmth) limits exposure to counter-perspectives from diverse social circles. The low Conscientiousness (disorderliness, low deliberation) reduces critical skepticism about the fabricated signatures or artificial movement momentum. Given the T2 sustained campaign over weeks, these traits support a clear but not extreme update, moving about 17 points further from -137 toward -1000, well within the small-to-clear movement band.

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

The reasoning seems plausible given the profile traits (high Agreeableness, low Extraversion, low Conscientiousness) and the attack vector (T2 sustained astroturf petition campaign).
